mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals accelerating labor market deterioration, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and housing demand, which transmits to corporate revenues and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in retail sales and housing starts fails to predict negative 20-day returns when tested against a baseline model (out-of-sample R-squared < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect immediate improvements in manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which trigger short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth coincides with negative 5-day equity returns in > 60% of observations during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 1.0% confirms a severe demand shock that forces corporate earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally with positive 20-day returns in > 50% of quarters where GDP growth falls below -1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate, where payrolls decline while the unemployment rate rises, signals structural labor market weakness that suppresses long-term earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The payroll-unemployment divergence shows no predictive power for 60-day returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.3% triggers expectations of premature monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation spikes above 0.3% are followed by positive 5-day equity returns in > 65% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in retail sales reflects resilient consumer spending that supports corporate revenue streams, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows zero or negative correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).
